explanation.sourceText: The English term 'father-in-law' combines 'father' with the preposition 'in-law', indicating a relationship by marriage rather than by blood.
explanation.targetText: Termenul englezesc „father-in-law” combină „father” (tată) cu prepoziția „in-law”, indicând o relație prin căsătorie, nu prin sânge.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word 'socru' derives from the Latin 'socrus', which in classical Latin meant sister-in-law (the wife of one's brother), but in Romanian shifted to mean father-in-law.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „socru” provine din latină, din termenul 'socrus', care în latină clasică însemna cumnată (soția fratelui), dar în română a căpătat sensul de tatăl soțului sau al soției.

examples.sourceText: The father-in-law often helps in the garden.
examples.targetText: Socru mă ajută des în grădină.

examples.sourceText: We visit my father-in-law on the weekend.
examples.targetText: Îl vizităm pe socrul meu în weekend.

examples.sourceText: The father-in-law has a lot of experience in craftsmanship.
examples.targetText: Socru are multă experiență în meserie.

learningTips.text: Remember 'father-in-law' by thinking of the 'law' part as meaning 'by law' or marriage, so it's the father related to you through marriage.
The Romanian word 'socru' sounds like 'so crew'—imagine your father-in-law as the 'crew' that supports your spouse.
Think of 'socru' as coming from Latin 'socrus', which originally meant sister-in-law, but in Romanian shifted to father-in-law—language can change in surprising ways!
To remember 'socru', associate it with the English word 'social'—your father-in-law is part of your social family circle.
